1. Buyer Pre-Offer Stage.Item: Check your finances and get a mortgage agreement in principle (AIP)
Once Address is Known, Even before Appointment, do the below
Research the area, distance from schools, flood chance (https://check-long-term-flood-risk.service.gov.uk/postcode), crime rates
Do subsidence checks, coal mine area, radon exposure checks, these are free to check online
Above line may look simple, but stuff like coal mine and subsidence checks in paid reports make or break a decision, why wait for solicitors to ask for it and pay for it
Download Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) online for free for the property, It shows AgeBand, when the property was built, and its built up area, energy rating

2. Viewing Checks with Agent.Item: Agents say we dont know to any of this, Solicitor will get this info, Just ask them you dont want to see the certificates, but absence of this means illegal constructions and council can fine you 50£ per day and ask you to demolish the extension, so you want to be sure about this before you make an offer !!!
Check if House has New WC Room Added, Ensuite, Kitchen, Bathroom extensions
Check for garage conversions/demolitions/conservatory extensions, some of these may not need planning permissions but may need building regulations
If yes, Check if seller has Planning Permissions and Building Completion Certificates for above
If No plans exist, Check for Reqularization certificate if work done without Planning Permission/Building Regulations
Check for FENSA certificates for glass doors/windows
If yes, check for boiler Gas Safety installation certificate
Check for rewiring/ electrical work done
If yes, check for rewiring EIC/EICR certificates

If Agents say no, Follow this up once Soicitor is instructed, mark it for follow up with solicitor, Mininum cost is 100 to 300£ for Solicitor instruction, ID checks, AML checks, and asking for these certificates, so you want to be sure about this before you make an offer !!!

3. Offer Acceptance Stage with Agent.Item: Agree on What is included in the sale (fixtures/fittings), This will need to be included in TA10 Form in future steps
Agree on deposit amount and date
1 -3 days, Client Care Pack Sent from Agent to Buyer (Signed)

Agree offer and acceptance terms, fixtures,fittings, deposit, completion date, and ask agent to send you a written confirmation of these details, this will be useful for solicitor instructions and future reference
Estate agent confirmation of agreed sale price
Register with conveyancer/solicitor - Cost 50-300£ Registration charges on Day 1 of instruction

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: Most of these documents are received on day of instruction or within 3 days from Seller's Solicitor to Buyer's Solicitor, They dont send to Buyer, we have to ask them to send over

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: Title Plan Contains a B/W map of the property and its boundaries
4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: Title Register Contains details of the property ownership and any restrictions or covenants
4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor
Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: Letter of Instruction (from Buyer Solicitor to Seller's Solicitor), Check your names spelling and address details are correct

TA6 Property Information Form, Very important form, contains details about the property, its boundaries, rights of way, disputes, services, and other important information. Review this form carefully and raise any questions or concerns with the seller's solicitor.
4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: TA6 Section 5.1 a) is for Windows Door Additions, b) for extensions, i) for any other alterations (eg Added Ensuite or WC ) , if any of these are yes,  and check if they have planning permissions and building regulations certificates, if not ask for regularization certificate

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Item: TA10 Fittings and Contents Form, this form should match what you agreed with agent
4. Buyer Solicitor Instructed.Initiator: Seller Solicitor sends to Buyer Solicitor

5. Buyer PreChecks Round 1 Negotiations.Item: If Extensions or alterations are present, request planning permissions and regulation certificates
Ask for FENSA certificates for New or replaced glass doors/windows, This is Structural integrity violation and can cause insurance and safety issues
Ask for boiler Gas Safety installation certificate
Negotiate on fixtures/fittings included based on above checks and other factors
Check for missing test and certifcates from above step
Calculate cost for tests or regularization of building planning permissions , FENSA, Gas Safety, EICR, and other certificates
Get Quotes from Builders for above missing certificates and regularization, and negotiate price reduction based on these costs
Negotiate price based on above checks and other factors

6. Survey & Reports.Item: Arrange basic valuation survey (usually required by mortgage lender) or they do online valuation nowadays
Buyer Paid 400 to 700£- Arrange RICS HomeBuyer report
Raise repairs/search queries with seller solicitor through our (buyer) solicitor
Review seller solicitor replies to above queries and survey report recommendations

7. Second Round- Survey based Negotiatio.Item: Negotiate price based on survey report recommendations and other factors

Check for additional test and certifcates for any issues found in survey report, eg damp, woodworm, roof issues, structural issues, and ask for these to be provided by seller solicitor if not already provided

Calculate cost for tests or regularization of building planning permissions , FENSA, Gas Safety, EICR, and other certificates, and negotiate price reduction based on these costs if these certificates are not provided by seller solicitor

8. Buyer Mortgage Application.Item: Submit mortgage application with survey report and other documents
Mortgage offer accepted and returned to lender

9. Order Paid Searches Now through Solic.Item: Local Authority Searches, Mandatory search that checks for planning permissions, building regulations, enforcement actions, and other local authority information related to the property
Environmental Searches (ground stability, flood risk, contaminated land), Mandatory- if Mortgaged
Water and Drainage Search (checks if property is connected to mains water and sewerage, and if there are any public drains on the property) Mandatory- if Mortgaged

10. Buyer Solicitor Tasks.Item: Analyses above paid search reports and creates a Report on Title (Read & Signed)
Mortgage offer received in Portal and checked
Mortgage offer accepted and returned to lender

11. Offers & Buyer Round 3 of Negotiatio.Item: Draft contract of sale and send to seller solicitor

12. Exchange & Completion.Item: Buildings Insurance active from exchange date
